title: Sustainable, Resilient, and Just Global Clean Energy Supply Chains
text:
Overview
This research theme aims at evidence-based analysis to understand the importance of a sustainable and resilient clean energy global supply chains to achieve climate goals. We evaluate the economic costs, carbon mitigation, air quality, and human health benefits achieved by global clean energy supply chains.

text:

title: Deep Policy Lab
text:
We use data-drive, evidence-base approach to study energy and climate policy and offer policy insights for energy decisions and climate actions to achieve carbon neutrality.




Learn more

title: About Deep Policy Lab
text:
The Deep Energy and Climate Policy Lab (Deep Policy Lab) provides data-driven, evidence based, energy and climate policy analysis and research to support energy decisions and climate actions.
Deep Policy Lab aims to advance actionable and evidence-based energy and climate solutions through data-driven analysis and dialogue. Deep Policy Lab leverages big data, AI, social sciences, and systems modeling to inform policy decisions on renewable energy, clean energy supply chains, deep decarbonization, and other topics related to energy transition and carbon neutrality.
Deep Policy Lab collaborates with academic institutions, government agencies, industry partners, and civil society organizations to foster innovation and impact in the field of energy and climate policy.

title: Power Systems Carbon Neutrality
text:
Overview
Electrifying energy, and decarbonizing electricity. Power system is at the center of global energy transtion. This research theme explores the pathways and their environmental, climate, human health, and socio-economic implications.

title: Power Systems Carbon Neutrality
text:
Featured publications

title: China Carbon Neutrality Initiative
text:
Overview
China is the world’s largest emitter of greenhouse gases. It has set a target to achieve carbon peak by 2030 and carbon neutrality by 2060. This initiative aims to facilitate the transition to a low-carbon economy in China to achieve the national carbon peak and carbon neutrality targets.

title: China Carbon Neutrality Initiative
text:

Nature Reviews Earth & Environment


In this Perspective, we summarize the key features of China’s CO2 emissions, its reduction processes and successes in meeting climate targets.

title: NYC Climate Week: Clean Energy Global Supply Chains Panel
text:
About
Global clean energy supply chains stand at a critical intersection of integration and decoupling. The panel will discuss the evolving landscape, benefits and challenges, and future directions of clean energy global supply chains, to meet climate goals while addressing growing international and domestic concerns.

title: Workshop on Open Modeling Carbon Neutrality of the Power Sector
text: Achieving China’s ambitious long-term climate goals will require fundamental transformations to the power sector. Many techno-economic uncertainties remain which will shape the pace and structure of this trajectory, including the costs of alternative energies, demand growth and electrification, flexibility of conventional generators, and deployment of firm low-carbon resources. In addition, policy evolution in the power sector, including provincial and regional electricity market developments and inter-provincial coordination, will have a large impact on which resources are selected and how they are operated. Open modeling efforts have been engaging with more open-source datasets, which if properly validated could provide a common framework for understanding the key features driving system evolution.

title: Workshop on Open Modeling Carbon Neutrality of the Power Sector
text: Achieving China’s ambitious long-term climate goals will require fundamental transformations to the power sector. Many techno-economic uncertainties remain which will shape the pace and structure of this trajectory, including the costs of alternative energies, demand growth and electrification, flexibility of conventional generators, and deployment of firm low-carbon resources. In addition, policy evolution in the power sector, including provincial and regional electricity market developments and inter-provincial coordination, will have a large impact on which resources are selected and how they are operated. Open modeling efforts have been engaging with more open-source datasets, which if properly validated could provide a common framework for understanding the key features driving system evolution.

title: Workshop on Open Modeling Carbon Neutrality of the Power Sector
text:
Objectives

Map the public domain scholarship on China’s power sector modeling in terms of models, data, and assumptions
Identify the set of assumptions (and their ranges) that have the largest impact on mid-century (2050/2060) outcomes across power sector models
Catalog open-source datasets as well as data processed from open sources used in power sector models, including sources, coverage and validation
Scope a research agenda for key unanswered questions regarding China’s power sector evolution

title: Workshop on Open Modeling Carbon Neutrality of the Power Sector
text:
Summary
I have co-organizied the “Workshop on Open Modeling Carbon Neutrality of the Power Sector” with Dr. Jiang Lin and Dr. Michael Davidson. The workshop was successfully held from July 19 to 20, 2024 at 241 Giannini Hall, University of California, Berkeley. Fifteen participants, including organizers and guests, joined in person, four participants joined via Zoom. Seven power system modeling teams, from both in the U.S. and China, presenting their modeling results. Dean David Ackerly of the College of Natural Resources welcomed the participants and highlighted the importance of the U.S. China collaboration. Professor Daniel Kammen joined the workshop by offering a remark on clean power transition in California and the U.S. during lunch time on July 19. The workshop created a platform for major power system modeling teams to introduce their models and share results. The participants also take focused sessions to discuss common challenges and emerging issues. The participants have good agreement to move forward on a few action items including a review paper and developing basic data transparency infrastructure.
